statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: This record is cross-layer verified and also aligned with a shipped one-click surface.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: ADMX_MicrosoftDefenderAntivirus SpynetReporting
links.summary: Microsoft maps SpyNetReporting to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet and documents 0 = disabled, 1 = basic membership, and 2 = advanced membership. The page also notes that on modern Windows builds value 1 or 2 can enroll the device into advanced membership behavior.

links.title: Local Defender MAPS lead note
links.summary: The local lead note tracks SpyNetReporting on HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet with 0, 1, and 2 as the documented values.

links.title: Win25H2Clean Procmon baseline for Defender MAPS policy path
links.summary: On the clean high-risk snapshot, SecurityHealthService.exe opened HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet and got NAME NOT FOUND, then fell back to HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet\SpyNetReporting and read Data: 2.

links.title: Win25H2Clean Procmon read for SpyNetReporting = 2
links.summary: After writing SpyNetReporting = 2 on the Defender Spynet policy path, SecurityHealthService.exe queried the same value and read Data: 2 directly from the policy branch.

links.title: Current security provider MAPS membership write
